data.current_description: Allows an authorized filer to transmit a SINGLE LIVE submission file. When an authorized filer transmits a filing to this resource it will transmit the file to EDGAR and return an accession number. EDGAR will then begin processing the submission.
data.summary_suggestion: Submit a single live filing to EDGAR
data.description_suggestion: Transmits a single live filing to EDGAR for processing. Requires Filer API Token and User API Token authentication. Returns HTTP 202 with one or more accession numbers upon successful transmission. Note: A 202 status indicates processing will begin; filings may still be suspended by EDGAR if they do not satisfy filing requirements. Use the Submission Status API with the returned accession number to monitor processing results. Filings must conform to SEC filing standards and technical specifications.

data.summary_suggestion: Submit a single test filing to EDGAR
data.description_suggestion: Transmits a single test filing to EDGAR for validation and testing. Requires Filer API Token and User API Token authentication. Returns HTTP 202 with one or more accession numbers upon successful transmission. Note: A 202 status indicates processing will begin; filings may still be suspended by EDGAR if they do not satisfy filing requirements. Test filing statuses are retained for two (2) days. Use the Submission Status API with the returned accession number to monitor processing results and identify any errors. Filings must conform to SEC filing standards and technical specifications.

data.current_description: When an authorized filer requests the status of multiple submissions that the filer made, the Check Multiple Submission Statuses API will respond with the current status of each, if available.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current processing status for multiple submissions by accession number. Requires Filer API Token authentication. Accepts a MultipleAccessionNumberRequest containing accession numbers to query. Returns HTTP 200 with status details for each submission, including processing state, errors, and final disposition when available. Detailed error information and filer notification text are only provided once processing is complete (when 'final' field is true). Status retention varies: TEST filings retained 2 days, LIVE filings retained 60 days, Suspended filings retained 6 days. Only submissions made by or delegated to the authenticated CIK can be queried.

data.current_description:
The request delegation invitations API provides the ability for an EDGAR account (for example, a filing agent) to request delegation from one
or more filers. If a filer accepts the delegation request, a delegation invitation will be sent from the filer to the EDGAR account requesting
delegation. If the delegation invitation is accepted by the EDGAR account requesting delegation, that EDGAR account will become a delegated
entity that can submit filings on behalf of the filer. This API will require the following inputs: the filer API token, the user API token,
the CIK belonging to the requesting EDGAR account that would like to become a delegated entity, and CIK(s) that will receive the delegation
request. The individual associated with the user API token must be an account administrator of the CIK requesting a delegation invitation.
The filer API token supplied for the request must match the CIK requesting a delegation invitation.

data.current_description:
The request delegation invitations API provides the ability for an EDGAR account (for example, a filing agent) to request delegation from one
or more filers. If a filer accepts the delegation request, a delegation invitation will be sent from the filer to the EDGAR account requesting
delegation. If the delegation invitation is accepted by the EDGAR account requesting delegation, that EDGAR account will become a delegated
entity that can submit filings on behalf of the filer. This API will require the following inputs: the filer API token, the user API token,
the CIK belonging to the requesting EDGAR account that would like to become a delegated entity, and CIK(s) that will receive the delegation
request. The individual associated with the user API token must be an account administrator of the CIK requesting a delegation invitation.
The filer API token supplied for the request must match the CIK requesting a delegation invitation.
data.description_suggestion: Allows an EDGAR account (such as a filing agent) to request delegation from one or more filers. Upon filer acceptance, the requesting account becomes a delegated entity authorized to submit filings on behalf of the filer.
